title: althaea oblongifolia
intro: Althaea oblongifolia, also known as the marsh mallow or marshmallow plant, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae). The plant's roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their mucilaginous properties, providing relief from inflammation, irritation, and dry coughs. The medicinal properties of Althaea oblongifolia include anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which make it beneficial for treating digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Althaea oblongifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or powders, and is also used in traditional medicine to create topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
